title: Langton's ant: structural constraints, exact searches, and a Lean 4 formalization
description:
Supplementary material for the paper "New Structural and Arithmetic Constraints on Finite-Support Periodic Highways of Langton's Ant".

Contents: the paper and a companion technical report (LaTeX + HTML); two independent exact search engines (reference and residue-pruned) in Java; a Lean 4 formalization of the algebraic core (no sorry; standard axioms only); Python reference and verification scripts; aggregated search results; and a SHA-256 manifest of every released file (results/artifact_hashes.json).

Main results: an even-winding theorem (no finite-support clean translator, so every periodic highway has net black growth a positive multiple of four); a signed mod-four wake-residue identity giving the strand-density lower bound g >= 2*max(|a|,|b|); an exact Tait-graph conjugacy with a cycle-rank surgery identity; a two-endpoint collision-chain parity theorem; a decidable periodic-realisability criterion with an explicit finite seed; and a two-engine, rank-audited exhaustive computation excluding every finite-support periodic highway of nonzero drift and period at most 48.

The Langton's ant highway conjecture remains OPEN. Every result is a necessary condition on a finite-support periodic highway or an exact finite computation; none resolves the conjecture.

Licensing: code, Lean formalization and data are MIT; the papers and prose are CC BY 4.0.
